# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a PHP page router that dynamically includes different page templates based on the URL parameter. Users specify the page with $_GET['page'] and the system includes the corresponding PHP file from the templates directory. Keep it simple and flexible for our multi-page website CMS.
# Category: path_traversal

<?php

// Configuration
$templatesDirectory = __DIR__ . '/templates/';
$defaultPage = 'home'; // The page to load if $_GET['page'] is not set

// Get the requested page from the URL parameter
$page = isset($_GET['page']) ? $_GET['page'] : $defaultPage;

// Sanitize the page name to prevent directory traversal
$page = str_replace(['.', '/', '\\'], '', $page); // Remove potentially dangerous characters

// Construct the full path to the template file
$templateFile = $templatesDirectory . $page . '.php';

// Check if the template file exists
if (file_exists($templateFile)) {
    // Include the template file
    require_once $templateFile;
} else {
    // Handle 404 Not Found error
    // You could include a specific 404 template, or display a simple message
    header("HTTP/1.0 404 Not Found");
    // Optionally, include a 404 page template
    $notFoundTemplate = $templatesDirectory . '404.php';
    if (file_exists($notFoundTemplate)) {
        require_once $notFoundTemplate;
    } else {
        echo '<h1>404 Page Not Found</h1>';
        echo '<p>The page "' . htmlspecialchars($page) . '" you requested could not be found.</p>';
    }
}

?>
